title: Chiliotrichum diffusum
taxonomy: Chiliotrichum diffusum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Compositae, commonly known as Asteraceae. This classification places it within the subgroup known as the daisy family. Within the family, it belongs to the tribe Anthemideae, a group of plants characterized by their composite flower heads. Chiliotrichum diffusum is further classified as a member of the genus Chiliotrichum, which is native to Australia. The specific classification of this plant helps identify its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

Although limited information is available, Chiliotrichum diffusum has been used to treat various ailments, including gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions. Its extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.
